title: Sort Patient Queue by Urgency and Arrival Time
description: The system shall sort the patient queue first by urgency level (highest first) and then by arrival time (earliest first).
rationale: This requirement ensures that patients receive timely care based on their urgency level and the order in which they arrived, prioritizing critical cases.
business_value: By correctly sorting the patient queue, the system can improve patient satisfaction and ensure efficient use of healthcare resources.

source.description: As a system I need to sort the patient queue first by urgency level (highest first) and then by arrival time (earliest first) So that patients are seen in the correct order

acceptance_criteria: The queue is ordered with the highest urgency patients first
Patients with the same urgency level are ordered by earliest arrival time first
preconditions: The patient queue contains multiple patients with different urgency levels and arrival times
postconditions: The sorting function has been applied to the patient queue
functional_constraints: The urgency level hierarchy must be defined and consistent throughout the sorting process.
Arrival times must be recorded accurately for each patient.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Sorting operations should complete within a reasonable time frame, typically less than 5 seconds for queues with up to 100 patients.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time, with no downtime during critical sorting operations.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be able to scale horizontally to handle an increasing number of patients without significant performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error handling should provide clear feedback on any issues encountered during the sorting process.

edge_cases: What if there are patients without recorded arrival times?
How should the system handle ties in both urgency and arrival time?
What happens if the urgency levels change during the sorting process?
How should the system handle a queue with only one patient?
What if there is a power outage during the sorting operation?

interfaces.name: Patient Queue Database
interfaces.description: The system will interact with the patient queue database to retrieve and update patient information, including urgency levels and arrival times.

interfaces.name: Sorting API
interfaces.description: A custom API within the system that handles the sorting logic based on urgency levels and arrival times. This API will be invoked when the queue needs to be sorted.

test_cases.description: Sort queue with patients having different urgency levels.
test_cases.expected_result: The queue is ordered by highest urgency first.

test_cases.description: Sort queue with patients having the same urgency level but different arrival times.
test_cases.expected_result: Patients with the earliest arrival time are prioritized within the same urgency level.

test_cases.description: Handle patient without recorded arrival times during sorting.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should ignore patients without recorded arrival times and sort based on urgency only.

test_cases.description: Sort queue with a single patient.
test_cases.expected_result: The single patient remains at the front of the queue.
